The brahmins had allegedly forcibly entered the home of the Scheduled Caste man who asked for money due to him. In the background, play out themes of movement and migration

title: Exclusive: How War In A Narrow Sea Could Reshape Global Economy Overnight
content: Geographically, the Strait of Hormuz is a narrow maritime corridor connecting the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea, and ultimately the Indian Ocean. Iran lies to the north, while Oman and the United Arab Emirates lie to the south.

title: Strait Of Hormuz: Iran's 50-Km-Wide Geopolitical Bargaining Chip
content: The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Office has said it received reports from merchant ships operating in the Arabian Gulf that the Strait of Hormuz has been closed. These reports could not be independently verified at this time, it said.

content: The US-Israeli strikes against Iran and reprisals by Tehran could severely disrupt the global supply of crude oil and send prices soaring to levels not seen in years.

content: In what could very well be a turning point in the history of Iran and the Middle East, US President Donald Trump claimed that Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was dead following the joint US-Israeli military operation against Tehran.

content: The facility is the highest rupee beneficiary of funds from the India Semiconductor Mission, with a 50% capital subsidy from the Union government, and a further 20% from the Gujarat government

title: Action to be taken against building lacking mandatory parking space

title: Peace meeting held in Bagalkot on Saturday after protest on Friday night
content: In Bagalkot, where residents staged protest on Friday night over the arrest of Hindutva activists, a conciliation peace meeting was held on Saturday in which P.C. Gaddigoudar, MP, former MLA Viranna Charantimath, senior police officials and residents of Killa Galli took part and it was resolved to maintain peace

title: ‘No Pongala should be offered on granite near Padmanabhaswamy temple entrances’
content: The superior quality granite that had been laid in the traditional style need to be preserved carefully, says a temple statement

title: II PU student dies of heart attack
content: In a tragic incident, a girl student who was about to appear for her II year PUC examination died of heart attack as she was getting ready to go to the examination hall in Belagavi district on Saturday.

title: Leaders in Jammu and Kashmir condemn U.S., Israel attack on Iran
content: Scores of protesters took to the streets in Kashmir’s Bandipora to show support for Iran and condemn the attack on it by raising pro-Iran slogans; similar scenes were witnessed in Budgam and Srinagar districts

title: Double life sentence for priest convicted in POCSO case
content: Priest accused of sexually assaulting a minor under the pretext of performing rituals to ‘drive away evil spirits’

title: In a first, fuel surcharge to be refunded to KSEB consumers
content: Monthly-billed consumers will be refunded 2 paise per unit and bi-monthly consumers, 1 paise per unit, through their bills for March

title: Congress mulls fielding AICC secretary Mathew Antony from Kanjirappally
content: The move comes amid speculations that the BJP plans to field Union Minister George Kurian from the Assembly seat; Mr. Antony is the AICC secretary in charge of Meghalaya, Mizoram, and Arunachal Pradesh

title: 'Delivering decisive action as directed': US releases new videos of strikes on Iran
content: US and Israel have launched coordinated strikes targeting Iran's leadership, reportedly killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his family. Operation Epic Fury, led by Donald Trump, utilized advanced weaponry including stealth jets and Tomahawk missiles to dismantle Iran's missile capabilities. The US Central Command confirmed the extensive military operation.

title: Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Bahrain, Kuwait: Gulf countries targeted by Iran after US-Israel strikes
content: Iran launched retaliatory attacks on US assets across the Gulf, targeting Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, and the UAE, following a joint US-Israel strike. The Revolutionary Guard threatened further "intense offensive operations" against Israel and US bases. The UAE reported one fatality and injuries, while Bahrain and Kuwait intercepted missiles, with Kuwait also experiencing a drone attack on its airport.

title: 'There was an explosion': PV Sindhu shares terrifying update from Dubai
content: Badminton star PV Sindhu is stranded in Dubai after missile-related airspace disruptions suspended flights.  Initially en route to the All England Open, she described a frightening explosion near the airport.  Thanks to airport staff and the Indian High Commission, she and her team are safe in a secure location, awaiting the situation to stabilize and hoping to reach England.

title: Bill Gates tells staff why he won't buy a $6bn 'toy' his late friend Paul Allen owned
content: Bill Gates clarified his philanthropic focus at a Gates Foundation town hall, stating he has no interest in purchasing the NFL franchise his late friend Paul Allen owned, despite its multi-billion dollar valuation. Gates emphasized his wealth is dedicated to the foundation, not sports team ownership.

title: 4-year-old girl mauled by a pack of stray dogs in Maharashtra town, sustains 100 wounds
content: A four-year-old girl suffered severe injuries after being attacked by stray dogs in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. The incident has sparked outrage and renewed calls for effective stray dog control measures, with residents alleging corruption in the municipal corporation's Animal Birth Control program. The child is currently in the ICU.

title: New SIM-binding rule from March 1: WhatsApp, Telegram users must know these changes
content: Starting March 1, messaging apps like WhatsApp and Telegram must implement SIM-binding, requiring services to function only with the registered SIM in the primary device. This government mandate aims to curb online fraud and enhance traceability by linking active accounts to verified SIMs. Web versions will also face stricter auto-logout rules.

title: Rashmika Mandanna turns wink girl and radiates bridal glow as she arrives in palki for wedding
content: New photos from Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's Udaipur wedding on February 26, 2026 reveal a regal celebration. The bride made a traditional palki entrance, as decor blended a 'Periodic Era' concept with deep Indian heritage. The couple observed Telugu Hindu and Kodava rites in two ceremonies, ahead of a March 4 Hyderabad reception. Read on to know more in detail.

title: ‘444 flights expected to be cancelled on Mar 1’: Aviation ministry shares update amid crisis
content: Over 410 domestic flights were cancelled on Saturday and 444 are expected on Sunday due to ongoing airspace restrictions over Iran and the Middle East. The Ministry of Civil Aviation is closely monitoring the situation, coordinating with airlines and airports to minimize passenger inconvenience and ensure safety.

title: Reza Pahlavi unveils democratic transition plan after Khamenei’s death
content: Iran's exiled crown prince, Reza Pahlavi, hailed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death following US-Israeli strikes, calling it the Islamic Republic's end. He praised President Trump and outlined a roadmap for a democratic transition, emphasizing unity among opposition groups and regional peace, including recognition of Israel. Pahlavi urged Iranians to prepare for widespread street presence.

title: H-1B visa data reveals the salary that OpenAI pays engineers, researchers in the US
content: Despite pledging to slow hiring, OpenAI is actively expanding its workforce, recruiting top talent from abroad. New H-1B visa filings reveal the AI lab offers exceptionally high base salaries, reflecting intense competition for skilled professionals. This surge in hiring, even with increased visa fees, underscores OpenAI's aggressive pursuit of AI expertise.

title: US-Israel strikes on Iran: How will India be hit by Strait of Hormuz closure? Explained
content: The Strait of Hormuz has Iran on the northern side and the UAE and Oman on the southern side. It extends for around 100 miles, and its narrowest stretch is 21 miles wide. It has shallow depth, which means that ships passing through it are vulnerable to naval mines.

title: Suicide drones, Tomahawks & more: The weapons that powered US-Israel strikes on Iran
content: Operation Epic Fury saw US forces launch a massive aerial assault on Iran, reportedly killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. The operation utilized Tomahawk missiles, F/A-18 and F-35 stealth fighters, and new one-way attack drones.  These precision strikes targeted key Iranian military projects, marking a significant escalation in regional tensions.

title: T20 World Cup 2026 upcoming matches: Full schedule, semifinal dates, venues
content: The ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 is nearing its conclusion, with England, South Africa, and New Zealand securing semifinal spots. The final berth will be decided between India and the West Indies in a crucial Super 8 clash. Semifinals are scheduled for March 4 and 5, with the final on March 8.

title: Fortnite Love and Legends Power Hour event: Release timings for all regions
content: Epic Games announced the final two Fortnite Love and Legends Power Hour events for February 28 and March 1, 2026. These events offer the most generous loot distribution, featuring four Legendary POIs per match with Rare Chests. Players have a final chance to earn Candy Hearts and themed cosmetic items during these limited-time, one-hour events.

title: Matthew Tkachuk calls Edmonton Oilers captain Connor McDavid best player in the world
content: Florida Panthers forward Matthew Tkachuk called Edmonton Oilers captain Connor McDavid the best player in the world after their Olympic gold medal showdown at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Games. Tkachuk reflected on a pivotal breakaway save by United States goaltender Connor Hellebuyck that preserved Team USA’s lead and shifted momentum in the championship game.

title: Toronto Maple Leafs face trade deadline pressure amid Auston Matthews scrutiny
content: As the NHL trade deadline approaches, the Toronto Maple Leafs are unsure about the direction of their roster and their captain, Auston Matthews; as rumors about NHL trades grow stronger, management needs to find a balance between being competitive in the short term and planning for the long term. This week is going to be very important for the NHL. Along with a lot of big news coming out. There are questions about player availability, locker room dynamics, and Matthews' future.

title: 'Gambled frequently, in debt': Man who killed wife, 3 kids held; police verify loan claim
content: A vegetable vendor, Munchun Kewat, has been apprehended in Rajasthan for allegedly murdering his wife and three daughters in Delhi's Samaypur Badli.  Initial reports suggest Kewat cited overwhelming debt and threats from a loan shark as motives.  Family members revealed he had borrowed heavily and gambled, leading to financial strain and arguments with his wife.

title: T20 World Cup: How an Indian throwdown specialist is boosting West Indies' power-hitters
content: Mumbai's side-arm throwdown specialist Abhishek Jain received a surprise call to bowl for the West Indies team during their practice session. Impressing coach Darren Sammy with his pace and accuracy, Jain was asked to be called daily for upcoming sessions. He also shared his gratitude towards KL Rahul for past support.

title: T20 World Cup: Why the spotlight is on Varun Chakravarthy
content: The tournament could well be divided into two contrasting halves for the mystery spinner: he enjoyed significant control and success in the group stages but Super 8 opponents South Africa and Zimbabwe handled him comfortably.

title: After Khamenei, who? How Iran chooses its Supreme Leader and who could be next
content: US President Donald Trump announced the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei following joint US-Israeli air strikes. Khamenei's demise plunges Iran into uncertainty, with President Pezeshkian and two other officials leading a transitional period. The article explores the succession process and potential successors, including Khamenei's son and grandson, alongside opposition figures advocating for democratic change.

title: IND vs WI: India won't 'mask pressure' even as Darren Sammy rallies a war cry
content: India head into a virtual quarter-final against West Indies at Eden Gardens determined to embrace, not mask, pressure. While Darren Sammy has issued a war cry, India’s management insists on reframing pressure as privilege. With South Africa unbeaten and a semi-final spot at stake, Sunday’s clash promises a high-stakes contest between two T20 heavyweights ready to fight fire with fire.

title: ‘In the name of Haidar’: Last Message posted on Khamenei’s X after Trump declares him dead
content: Following Donald Trump's announcement of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death in a US-Israel strike, Iran's Supreme Leader's social media posted a cryptic message invoking Haidar. Iran has not confirmed the death but vowed retaliation, launching missiles and drones at Israel and US bases. Khamenei, ruling since 1989, leaves no confirmed successor, raising concerns about political stability.

title: Delhi excise case: HC to hear CBI plea against Kejriwal, Sisodia's discharge on March 9
content: The CBI has appealed the Delhi High Court against a trial court's decision to discharge all 23 accused, including Arvind Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia, in the Delhi excise policy case. The trial court found no prima facie case of conspiracy or criminal intent, criticizing the prosecution's reliance on conjecture. The High Court will hear the CBI's challenge on March 9.

title: Batting order not fixed, says India coach ahead of must-win West Indies clash
content: Tilak had a modest run in the first five matches of the tournament while batting at one down, managing a strike-rate of 120. The team management then pushed him to No. 6 against Zimbabwe.

title: Watch: Drone debris hits Dubai’s Burj Al Arab after Iranian strike
content: Dubai's iconic Burj Al Arab faced a minor fire after debris from an intercepted drone struck its facade. Authorities confirmed the incident, with Civil Defence swiftly controlling the blaze. This follows reports of an Iranian drone strike and damage at Dubai International Airport, prompting residents to seek shelter due to potential missile threats.

title: UAE shuts classrooms: All schools & universities go online amid security concerns
content: Amidst escalating regional tensions involving Iran, the US and Israel, the UAE has temporarily moved all schools and universities to online learning from March 2-4, 2026. This precautionary measure aims to ensure student and staff safety while maintaining educational continuity, leveraging digital infrastructure built during the pandemic. Other Gulf nations are also adopting similar strategies.

title: Khamenei killed in US strike: Who are his potential successors?
content: US President Donald Trump announced Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei's death following joint US-Israeli strikes, calling it a chance for Iranians to reclaim their nation.  Israeli officials confirmed the death, with Iranian state media reporting explosions and disruptions in Tehran.  The succession process, tightly controlled by the regime, now faces scrutiny amid regional tensions.

title: All you need to know about ‘Cobra’ actor Vikram's home in Chennai
content: Chiyaan Vikram, the acclaimed Tamil actor, embraces a simple, eco-conscious life at his Chennai home in Mahalingapuram. The residence features a minimalist design and natural materials—teakwood and marble—framed by garden pockets and inner courtyards that cultivate serenity and sustainability. His deep commitment to his craft complements a grounded personal life.

title: Bengaluru school owner held for 'raping' Class 8 student; principal among 3 arrested
content: A couple running a Devanahalli residential school and its principal were arrested under the Pocso Act following a class 8 student's sexual assault allegations. The 14-year-old survivor accused the owner of rape and claimed his wife and the principal failed to intervene, dismissing her concerns as common. Police are investigating further incidents.

title: Operation Epic Fury: How Trump-led US strikes in Tehran killed Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei
content: Operation Epic Fury, a US military action under Donald Trump, achieved its main goal. Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in strikes on Tehran. The operation also targeted Khamenei's daughter and grandchild. The offensive marked a major escalation, aiming to dismantle the Khamenei-led establishment. Explosions were reported across several Iranian cities.

title: Exiled crown prince hails ‘turning point’ after Ayatollah’s death - top developments
content: A massive cloud of smoke rose over Tehran following a daylight "preventive attack" by the United States and Israel, targeting military sites near Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's offices. Loud blasts were heard as the US confirmed its participation in the strikes, hours after President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with nuclear talks and hinted at a "big decision"

title: Iran-Israel: Drone hits residential building in Bahrain- video
content: Bahrain's capital Manama faced attacks on residential buildings. An Iranian drone struck a tower in the city. This follows Iran's missile strikes on United States military bases in Bahrain and other Gulf nations. The strikes were in retaliation for joint US-Israel airstrikes on Tehran. Civil defense teams are responding to the incidents.

title: India could soon get its first openly queer MP
content:
Himanshi Dhawan is a Senior Editor with The Times of India with 25 years of experience in reporting on politics, social sector, gender and marginalised communities. Her writing spans issues related to human rights violations, and looking at health, education, and livelihoods from a gendered lens. In recognition for her reporting she has received the UNFPA-Population First Laadli award for gender sensitive reporting in 2010-11. She has also been the recipient of the Edward Murrow Fellowship (2007) by the US State Department’s International Visitor Leadership Program. 
She has studied English Literature at St Stephen's College, DU and completed her post-graduation in mass communication from Sophia College, Mumbai. Read More

title: Matt Holliday's Stillwater estate listed for $12 million
content: a Stillwater, Oklahoma, home that once belonged to an MLB champion and seven-time All-Star is on the market at $12 million . the home features sports-related amenities such as a professionally lit baseball field in the front yard, a putting green in the backyard, and a $2 million, two-level swimming pool . indoor batting cages, pickleball and basketball courts reside in a detached $150,000 guest home on the property .

title: 20 dead, most of them women, in Andhra Pradesh's worst firecracker unit explosion
content: the toll could rise as many of the injured were unlikely to survive . most of the workers in the unit were women from nearby villages, but their exact number is not known . the highest toll recorded at a firecracker unit blast in the state was in 2014 when 11 people were killed .

content: ESPN obtained the results of the NFL Players Association's 2026 report cards . it grades franchises from A-plus to F-minus on everything from ownership to treatment of families . all players who were on a 2025 roster at the time of the survey were eligible to participate .

title: The game that will define the 2026 season for every power-conference team
content: the season-defining game will be for all 68 power-conference teams in 2026 . peruse BC's schedule given last year's struggles, and it's tough to find many wins . the Eagles open on the road at Cincinnati, then play FCS Maine .

title: Power Rankings: Stakes are high for UCLA, two teams rejoin the list
content: the Bruins will be just the fourth program to finish with a perfect regular season in the Big Ten women's college basketball . the last to do it was Maryland in 2015, the season the Terps joined the big Ten and advanced to the final Four for the second year in a row .
